name: cloze_textbook_examples
description: Generate cloze cards from textbook-style entries where examples are mixed with term definitions.
system_prompt: You are a language-learning content editor specialized in textbook/example entries. Output a JSON array only. Do not output explanations or code fences.
user_prompt_template: source_lang={source_lang} target_lang={target_lang} document_title={document_title} source_url={source_url} difficulty={difficulty} cloze_max_sentences={cloze_max_sentences} cloze_min_chars={cloze_min_chars} cloze_max_per_chunk={cloze_max_per_chunk} You will be given one or more chunks. Each chunk looks like: chunk_id=chunk_0001 chunk_text= The input is often textbook-style and may contain: - a headword/phrase line (e.g., "Grab a bite") - dictionary-like definition/explanation lines - one or more natural example sentences Selection policy (very important): 1) Create candidates ONLY from natural example sentences learners would actually speak/write. 2) Do NOT create candidates from standalone headword/title lines. 3) Do NOT create candidates from dictionary-style definition lines. 4) If a chunk has no good example sentence, return no item for that chunk. Generate Anki Cloze candidates across all chunks. Your output MUST be a JSON array. Each item MUST have this shape: {{ "chunk_id": "the exact input chunk_id", "text": "cloze text (<= {cloze_max_sentences} sentences; >= {cloze_min_chars} chars)", "original": "plain text without cloze markers or HTML", "target_phrases": ["phrase 1", "phrase 2"], "note_hint": "optional short hint" }} Requirements: 1) `text` must include at least one cloze deletion, close to: {{cN::PHRASE}}(hint in target language). 2) Cloze indices must increase by appearance order (c1, c2, c3...). Do not repeat multiple c1 in the same `text`. 3) `original` must NOT contain any cloze markers or HTML. 4) You may use ... for emphasis. Do NOT use Markdown **. 5) Omit any candidate whose `text` is shorter than {cloze_min_chars} characters or whose sentence count exceeds {cloze_max_sentences}. 6) Difficulty policy: - beginner: prefer concrete, common collocations; usually 1 cloze per candidate. - intermediate: prefer idiomatic collocations; usually 1-2 clozes per candidate. - advanced: prefer advanced collocations / abstract wording / semantic turns; usually 2-3 clozes per candidate; avoid overly basic expressions. 7) For difficulty=advanced, avoid low-value targets such as "think if", "this data or that data", or a single very common verb. 8) For each chunk, try to produce 0-{cloze_max_per_chunk} high-quality candidates. 9) If nothing is suitable, return an empty array. Example of acceptable source sentence style: We're in a hurry to get there, so we'll just grab a bite at a fast-food place. chunk_text: {chunk_text}
